questions.question: What is the main problem with traditional bridges for cross-chain payments?
options: They take 5-15 minutes and stack multiple fees (source gas + bridge fee + destination gas + swap fee)
questions.correctAnswer: They take 5-15 minutes and stack multiple fees (source gas + bridge fee + destination gas + swap fee)

questions.question: How does only swaps differ from traditional bridges?
options: Solvers provide instant liquidity by sending tokens on the destination chain immediately
It only works for small amounts
questions.correctAnswer: Solvers provide instant liquidity by sending tokens on the destination chain immediately

questions.question: In the CrossChainPaymentGateway contract, all merchants settle to the same chain and token configured at deployment.
Why are the router, settlementChainId, and settlementToken variables declared as immutable?
options: To save gas and enforce consistency across all merchants
To allow merchants to change settings
questions.correctAnswer: To save gas and enforce consistency across all merchants

questions.question: The Payment struct stores a ______ which links back to the only swaps router to check if the swap executed.
Why must merchants register before receiving payments?
options: To prevent accidental payments to typos and give merchants control over settlement address
questions.correctAnswer: To prevent accidental payments to typos and give merchants control over settlement address

questions.question: A customer can make a payment on the same chain where settlements occur (e.g., pay on Base when Base is the settlement chain).
